# Efficiency — was this built in the most efficient way?

**Verdict:** ship — No efficiency defect carries any security or correctness impact; the single headline cost is the already-deferred per-fragment Chromium launch (SB1), and the in-scope deterministic engine is linear, single-pass, and correctly amortized.

## Summary

The deterministic core was built efficiently. The HTML allowlist tokenize/serialize, WCAG contrast math, and auditor mapping are single-pass, iterative (explicit stacks, not recursion), and free of any algorithmic (O(n²)) defect — per-fragment CPU is linear in fragment size with small constants. The startup/lifecycle wiring is also sound: a single warm Ollama sidecar with 24h keep-alive, a lazily-opened SQLite DB, and a once-built shared FTS5 retriever, all amortized across the whole session rather than rebuilt per turn. Every confirmed finding is a **low- or info-severity micro-optimization with zero security or correctness dimension** under the single-user on-device threat model. The one cost that actually matters to perceived latency — a full Chromium cold-launch plus an unconditional 1000ms settle paid per audited fragment — is the already-known, deferred SB1 item being quantified here, not a new defect. Nothing blocks ship.

## Confirmed findings

Ordered by adjusted severity. Note that nothing rose above **low**; the verification process deliberately downgraded performance observations that have no adversarial vector on a single-user on-device app.

| Severity | Title | Location | Recommendation |
|---|---|---|---|
| low | **Render harness dominates all engine cost** — per-fragment Chromium cold-launch + unconditional 1000ms settle (this is the deferred **SB1** item, merging three sub-findings) | `src/engine/render/playwright-runner.ts:142-196` (launch :153, newContext :155, settle :145/:158, close :192); callers `src/runtime/app-api.ts:370-375` (build loop) and `:412-429` (remediate, up to 5×) | Reuse one long-lived browser owned by the runner (newContext/newPage per fragment, close only on app shutdown — mirror the Ollama/docling attach-spawn-stop lifecycle). Make the settle adaptive (`waitForLoadState('networkidle')` or a short delay) or conditional on image/web-font fragments. **Only ~300-800ms launch+teardown per fragment is recoverable by pooling**; the 1000ms settle is a separate, already-configurable (`RENDER_SETTLE_DELAY_MS`) per-page cost. The deterministic engine math (tokenize/serialize, contrast) is microseconds-to-ms by comparison. |
| low | **Remediate re-runs the full gate on identical HTML** with no content-keyed memoization | `src/runtime/app-api.ts:412-429`; `gate.ts:70-92` has no cache | Memoize `enforceGate` by a hash of the input HTML for the duration of one remediate flow; short-circuit the `?? sourceHtml` echo case to reuse the already-computed `before` result. A pure no-op remediate currently renders byte-identical HTML twice; worst case is ≤5 renders. Each redundant render is a full Chromium launch (stacks on SB1). |
| low | **Per-pixel `**2.4` (pow) in the image-sampling loop** — the only O(pixels) hot path in the engine | `src/engine/render/sample.ts:15-21,39-49` (same `**2.4` pattern, non-hot, at `src/engine/contrast.ts:138-145`) | Add a module-level 256-entry `LINEAR` LUT (channels are `Uint8` 0-255 per `png.ts:13,61`, so the pow has exactly 256 outputs/channel — the LUT is exact) and hoist `width*height` out of the loop condition. Existing unit tests pin the numeric outputs. Fires only for text over a background image and is dwarfed by the per-fragment screenshot it sits inside — marginal payoff. |
| low | **Production build emits/ships `.d.ts` and compiles all test files** (one tsconfig for typecheck + ship) | `tsconfig.json:15-16,19` (declaration/sourceMap true, no test exclude); `package.json:13` (`build` = bare `tsc`) | Add a `tsconfig.build.json` (extends base with `declaration:false`, `sourceMap:false`, `exclude:["**/*.test.ts"]`) and point `npm run build` at it; keep the strict base for typecheck/test. Measured: 82 dead `.d.ts` (~120 KB) ship inside the asar (electron-builder globs lack `!**/*.d.ts`), plus 162 test artifacts compiled per build. Negligible bytes next to the bundled multi-GB Ollama/docling sidecars; build-hygiene only. |
| low | **`DEFAULT_SYSTEM_PROMPT` exported but unused in the turn pipeline** and duplicates the (already-drifted) per-mode `HARD_RULES` | `src/runtime/app-api.ts:142-151`; `src/orchestrator/modes.ts:19-27`; re-export `src/runtime/index.ts:10` | Delete the constant and its re-export; make `HARD_RULES`/`systemPromptForMode` the single source of truth. The live resolver `baseSystemFor` never references it. The two copies have already diverged (the dead one enumerates the tool list, HARD_RULES does not), reinforcing the drift risk. Safety is unaffected — the live per-mode prompt plus the unconditional server-side gate are the enforcement loci. |
| info | **Gradient/image contrast path re-composites + re-parses the constant foreground per candidate, and round-trips numeric candidate colors through `rgb(...)` strings** (merges two sub-findings) | `src/engine/render/run-contrast.ts:37-51` (worstAgainst), `:64-77` (gradientCandidates); `src/engine/contrast.ts:152-168,259-276` | Parse `fg`'s alpha once before the loop in `worstAgainst`; if opaque, reuse a precomputed `fgSolid` + its luminance for all candidates (the opaque composite is a verified no-op). Thread an internal numeric `Rgb`/`Rgba` between gradientCandidates/compositeLayers/luminance so candidates never become strings (each is currently serialized once and re-parsed twice). Output is identical; pure microsecond waste, de-duped upstream by the `seen` set. |
| info | **Two regex constructions inside per-iteration loops** | `src/engine/allowlist.ts:446` (`urlRe` literal per style declaration, loop from :437) and `:334` (`closeRe` built per raw-text open tag) | Optional: hoist a module-level `URL_SCHEME_RE` and reset `lastIndex` (or `matchAll`) in filterStyle; precompute a 2-entry `Map<rawTextTag,RegExp>` for the close-tag scan (the :334 regex is `tag`-interpolated, so it can't be a single static literal). Neither is a high-volume path; immeasurable. |
| info | **PNG decoder allocates a fresh row buffer per scanline and branches on the row-constant filter type per byte** | `src/engine/render/png.ts:64-89` (alloc :67, per-byte `if/else` chain :74-77) | Optional: double-buffer two `Uint8Array(stride)` and swap prev/cur each row; `switch(filter)` once per row. Constant-factor only — the decode is a correct single-pass O(pixels) algorithm and the branch is near-perfectly predicted. Fires solely for text over a background image (clipped screenshot region). |
| info | **WAL enabled but `synchronous` left at FULL** — every commit fsyncs | `src/storage/database.ts:28-29` (only PRAGMAs are `journal_mode=WAL` + `foreign_keys=ON`) | Add `PRAGMA synchronous = NORMAL` (documented safe+fast WAL pairing); optionally `busy_timeout`/`cache_size`. FULL is a safe, *more*-durable conservative default — not a defect; benefit is negligible at this single-user write volume (session appends, brand-kit saves, migrations). |
| info | **Statements re-prepared (recompiled) on every call** in the DB wrapper and retriever | `src/storage/database.ts:38,41,45`; `src/knowledge/retriever.ts:150` (routeRubric), `:165` (searchFts) — only the INSERT is prepared once (`:128`) | Add a small prepared-statement cache (Map keyed by SQL) in the DB wrapper; hoist the retriever's two SELECTs to construction time. Microseconds against turn latency dominated by local Ollama inference; never user-observable. |
| info | **Orchestrator + tool registry + engine-deps reconstructed every turn** though all inputs are stable for the app lifetime | `src/runtime/app-api.ts:314-320` (buildOrchestrator), called per-turn `:354`,`:391`; `src/runtime/deps.ts:97-124` | Build the stateless Orchestrator (deps + registry) once inside `createAppApi` and reuse it — `handleTurn` is stateless and all captured inputs are bound once. DRY/cleanliness only; the one expensive component (the FTS5 retriever) is already shared. Latent footgun: `deps.ts:99 opts.retriever ?? createRetriever()` would build a second index, but has no live caller today. |

## Checked & cleared / refuted

- **REFUTED — "FTS5 index build inserts each unit in its own implicit transaction at startup"** (`src/knowledge/retriever.ts:128-136`): the loop does lack a `BEGIN/COMMIT`, but the impact is non-existent — it is a one-time build of **12** bundled units into an `:memory:` table (no fsync, fixed tiny N at ~3.8 KB). Wrapping it in a transaction is harmless but immeasurable. Not a genuine finding. *(Side note: an earlier pass counted "15 units" by including the 3 pack-level `id` fields; the true unit count is 12.)*
- **HEALTHY (confirmed, no action) — deterministic engine is single-pass and linear**: `repairAllowlist` runs tokenize → some(h1) → buildTree → transform → serialize exactly once each; tokenize is one monotonic pass with attrs/text decoded once; transform/serialize use explicit stacks (the C13 fix), not recursion; auditor/mapping/canvas-shell are flat loops over a module-level constant CSS (`src/engine/allowlist.ts:208-348,365-389,551-610,615-626`; `auditor.ts:37-68`; `mapping.ts:35-89`; `canvas-shell.ts:23-55`). Two *latent* worst-case quadratic micro-edges exist (the close-tag backward stack scan at `allowlist.ts:378-385`; the raw-text `html.slice(i)` at `:335`) but never manifest on bounded single-page Canvas fragments (RAW_TEXT_TAGS are only script/style, both dropped) and sit behind the sandboxed iframe + CSP — fidelity gaps, not efficiency defects.
- **HEALTHY — Ollama warm-keep posture is correct**: model resident before first request, 24h `keep_alive` re-asserted per `/api/chat` request (`payload.ts:109`) so it holds even in attach mode, and model de-dup (one tag for all roles) avoids per-role reloads (`config.ts:56,61`; `process.ts:119-135`; `sidecar.ts:46-49`). No cold-load on first turn.
- **HEALTHY — startup wiring is amortized, not per-turn**: the FTS5 index, SQLite DB, knowledge packs, and the shared Ollama sidecar are each built/opened exactly once per app instance and reused across turns (`src/runtime/app-api.ts:256-311`, retriever at `:263`; `src/knowledge/retriever.ts:109-191`; single `createAppApi` via `main.ts:70` → `build-api.ts:13-24`). No premature abstraction; no redundant spawns or eager I/O. The known-open daemon crash-respawn gap is correctly out of this lens.
- **Out of scope but already assessed**: the dev-only esbuild HIGH advisory does not ship (prod runtime deps are only axe-core + playwright), so it carries no runtime efficiency or security weight for the distributed app.

## Action items before ship

None are ship-blocking. Prioritized; each optimization has a cheap guard test noted.

1. **(low — defer to when SB1 lands)** Pool one Chromium across a turn's gate loop and the remediate re-audit loop; close on app shutdown. Biggest perceived-latency win. — `playwright-runner.ts`, `app-api.ts`. *Guard test: audit K>1 fragments, assert `launchImpl` invoked once (inject a launch seam + count), plus a context-isolation test that fragment N can't see N-1's DOM.* [medium]
2. **(low)** Lower/condition the 1000ms settle default — static gated fragments are paint-stable after `load` (animations/transitions disabled in `canvas-shell.ts:24`, system fonts only, scripts stripped); keep a small margin only for image-bearing fragments. Independent of SB1, stacks with it. — `playwright-runner.ts:145,158`. *Guard test: static fragment with settle override asserts `waitForTimeout` not called / called with 0.* [cheap]
3. **(low)** Content-key memoize `enforceGate` within a remediate flow + short-circuit the echo case. — `app-api.ts:412-429`. *Guard test: scripted no-op repair asserts the injected audit dep runs once, not twice, for unchanged HTML.* [cheap]
4. **(low)** Add `tsconfig.build.json` (no declarations/sourcemaps, exclude tests); point `npm run build` at it. *Guard test: `find dist -name '*.d.ts' -o -name '*.test.js'` is empty after build.* [cheap]
5. **(low)** Delete the dead `DEFAULT_SYSTEM_PROMPT` duplicate and its re-export. [trivial]
6. **(info — optional micro-cleanups, do only if profiled hot)** 256-entry luminance LUT in `sample.ts`/`contrast.ts`; `PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL`; prepared-statement cache; hoist the opaque-fg short-circuit in `worstAgainst`; build the Orchestrator once in `createAppApi`. All reproduce identical outputs and are pinned by existing tests. [cheap/trivial]
